anthropic.note: Enforced via daily lockfile /tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt — at most one audit fire per UTC day. Each fire = 1 Opus extended-thinking call (~$2). No Haiku callers in active code; if reintroduced, pair with explicit enforcement and update this cap.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.gemini:
**Reasoning:** Static analysis shows 0 calls despite 20 timers; event-driven calls are completely unmonitored. Anthropic’s limit uses `/tmp/`, which erases on container restarts. *Forecast if my analysis is wrong:* Minor local file clutter. *If right:* Infinite budget drain.

**a) Runaway loop:** Container crash-loops constantly wipe `/tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt`. Unmonitored timers endlessly fire $2 Opus calls.
**b) Missing caps:** Zero provider-level API hard-stops. Limits rely strictly on easily-bypassed local state.
**c) 2x Blow-up:** If Gemini runs 2x (e.g., 100 calls), it hits the local cap safely. But if Anthropic timers fire 2x during a `/tmp/` failure, costs compound exponentially at $2/call.
**d) Invisible burn:** Anthropic. Background restarts silently clear the lockfile, b
ai_consensus.grok:
**Risk Audit:**

(a) Runaway-loop: /tmp lockfile can be deleted on restart/container recycle → any of the 20 timers could fire continuous Opus calls in error-retry loops.

(b) Missing daily caps: Gemini cap only guards 2 functions; any new caller bypasses it. No cap on potential Haiku reintroduction.

(c) 2x rate: Anthropic at 2 calls/day = $1,460/yr (vs current $730 cap). Gemini 2x is still cheap.

(d) Stealth burner: **Anthropic** — $2 Opus charges via silent daily timer will only appear on monthly bill; lockfile gives zero visibility when it fails.

Recommend replacing /tmp lock with atomic Redis counter before launch.
